texto: As we learn to give thanks for all of life and death, for all of this given world of ours, we find a deep joy. It is the joy of trust, the joy of faith in the faithfulness at the heart of all things. It is the joy of gratefulness in touch with the fullness of life.
explicacao: Citação de David Steindl-Rast sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “As we learn to give thanks for all of life and death, for all of this given world of ours, we fin…” articula gratidao, gratefulness, gratitude em registro de gratidao, com relevância para contextos como luto e gratitude.

frase_original: As we learn to give thanks for all of life and death, for all of this given world of ours, we find a deep joy. It is the joy of trust, the joy of faith in the faithfulness at the heart of all things. It is the joy of gratefulness in touch with the fullness of life.

texto: There is a wave of gratefulness because people are becoming aware how important this is and how this can change our world. It can change our world in immensely important ways, because if you're grateful, you're not fearful, and if you're not fearful, you're not violent. If you're grateful, you act out of a sense of enough and not of a sense of scarcity, and you are willing to share. If you are grateful, you are enjoying the differences between people, and you are respectful to everybody, and that changes this power pyramid under which we live.
explicacao: Citação de David Steindl-Rast sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “There is a wave of gratefulness because people are becoming aware how important this is and how t…” articula gratidao, fear, gratitude em registro de gratidao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e gratitude.

It can change our world in immensely important ways, because if you're grateful, you're not fearful, and if you're not fearful, you're not violent. If you're grateful, you act out of a sense of enough and not of a sense of scarcity, and you are willing to share. If you are grateful, you are enjoying the differences between people, and you are respectful to everybody, and that changes this power pyramid under which we live.

texto: Can you be grateful for everything? No. But in every moment.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de David Steindl-Rast que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Can you be grateful for everything? No. But in every moment.”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre gratidao, gratitude, reflexao, com foco em reflexao.
